page: Amazon Web Services i8ge.3xlarge is a storage-optimized instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor and an ARM64-based AWS Graviton processor. It features 12 dedicated physical cores using the ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 architecture running at 2.8 GHz, with 1 thread per core. The processor includes 64 KB of L1d cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and 36 MB of L3 cache. The instance is equipped with 96.0 GB of system memory, providing a resource density of 8.0 GB per core. It includes a bundled 7500 GB local NVMe SSD storage array and a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier memory performance, with the Passmark memory score placing in the top 10% and large-block uncached read and write memory bandwidth testing in the top 25%. Single-core performance is strong, as demonstrated by Geekbench single-core and text processing scores in the top 25%, alongside strong single-threaded bzip3 decompression. Multi-core performance, static web serving, and Redis SET operations align with average middle-tier expectations. However, cryptographic performance is weak, with the Passmark CPU encryption test scoring in the bottom 25%, and LLM inference tasks show mixed to weak performance.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the i8ge.3xlarge offers high resource density by combining a substantial 7500 GB local NVMe SSD storage capacity with a generous 96.0 GB memory allocation on an ARM64 architecture. This profile makes the instance highly suitable for storage-intensive and database workloads, such as high-throughput transactional databases, caching layers, and data indexing. It is also well-suited for memory-sensitive tasks that benefit from its top-tier memory bandwidth, though it is less optimal for compute-heavy cryptographic operations or large-scale LLM inference.
description: Amazon Web Services i8ge.3xlarge is a storage-optimized instance featuring 12 dedicated physical cores powered by an ARM64 AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor running at 2.8 GHz. It is equipped with 96.0 GB of system memory and a bundled 7500 GB local NVMe SSD. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, the instance delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps. Benchmark evaluations highlight top-tier memory performance, with Passmark memory scores in the top 10% and strong large-block memory bandwidth. Single-core processing and text manipulation also rank in the top 25%, though cryptographic operations and LLM inference show weaker relative performance. This hardware configuration provides a highly efficient balance of dense local storage and memory, making it an optimal fit for transactional databases, caching systems, and indexing workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services i8ge.3xlarge is a storage-optimized instance featuring 12 dedicated ARM64 Graviton cores, 96 GB of RAM, and a bundled 7500 GB local NVMe SSD for high-throughput database workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services i8ge.3xlarge features 12 ARM64 Graviton cores, 96 GB RAM, and a 7500 GB NVMe SSD, optimized for storage and database workloads.
tagline: An ARM64 storage-optimized instance combining a massive local NVMe SSD with top-tier memory bandwidth for data-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 12 dedicated ARM64 AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 physical cores running at 2.8 GHz
Bundled 7500 GB local NVMe SSD storage for high-capacity, low-latency data access
96.0 GB of system memory with top-tier memory bandwidth performance
12 Gbps baseline network bandwidth powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor
Best suited for transactional databases, caching layers, and indexing workloads
